additionalTime: 72 hours fermentation plus 3 to 4 hours final proof
author: Baking Steel
description: Baking Steel's printable 72-hour pizza dough formula with a 24-hour room-temperature rise, 48 hours in the refrigerator, and a covered room-temperature proof after balling.
equipment: Covered dough container or plastic wrap
instructions: Add 500 grams bread flour to a large bowl.
Scatter 1 gram instant yeast over the flour and whisk until evenly distributed.
Add 16 grams sea salt and whisk again.
Mix by hand or with a dough whisk until the flour is fully hydrated.
Knead by hand in the bowl for 2 to 3 minutes, until the dough comes together and feels slightly sticky.
Cover the bowl and let the dough rise at room temperature for 24 hours.
Transfer the covered dough to the refrigerator for 48 hours.
Remove the dough from the refrigerator, divide it into 2 pieces, and shape each piece into a dough ball.
Proof the dough balls covered at room temperature for 3 to 4 hours before using.
notes: Formula from Baking Steel's printable 72-hour pizza dough recipe.
The source yield is 2 dough balls for 12- to 14-inch pizzas.
This is the printable scratch formula, not the larger packaged dough mix formula.
title: Baking Steel 72-Hour Pizza Dough
totalTime: 15 minutes active, plus 72 hours fermentation and 3 to 4 hours final proof
